summary: Tested Phase 3 features: multi-currency finance (manual per-transaction INR/USD conversion), expense tracker with 3 types + breakdown chart, private per-user Notes, static Help page, and regression on currency-touched backend routers (clients.py, portal.py, dashboard.py, finance.py, settings.py). Created backend/tests/test_phase3_features.py (14 tests, all passing) plus full Playwright UI verification of Finance (add/delete USD+INR expenses, breakdown pie chart), Invoices (USD invoice creation + detail page currency note), Notes (create/edit/pin/delete), Help (accordion + search filter), Settings currency dropdown, Clients/Analytics/Portal currency displays. Full regression smoke test of Dashboard, Pipeline, Projects, Tasks, Knowledge Base, Password Vault, Command Palette, Notifications passed with no console errors. All test data (leads/clients/projects/invoices/expenses/notes/portal users) cleaned up post-testing - DB confirmed back to 0 across all finance collections.

minor.issue: Pre-existing (not introduced this session): CORS_ORIGINS="*" wildcard even though cookies (credentials) are used. Flagged again for visibility, recurring from prior iterations 1-3.

minor.endpoint: No DELETE endpoint for portal/client-role users
minor.issue: clients.py has POST /{client_id}/portal-user to create a portal login, but there's no corresponding DELETE endpoint to remove it. When a client is deleted, any associated portal user account is orphaned in the users collection. Had to clean up via direct DB query during test cleanup. Minor, low priority - not a regression, pre-existing gap.

action_items: No blocking action required - all Phase 3 features working correctly.
Optional/low priority: add DELETE /api/clients/{client_id}/portal-user endpoint (or cascade-delete on client deletion) to avoid orphaned client-role user accounts.
Optional/low priority (recurring, pre-existing): tighten CORS_ORIGINS from wildcard to explicit origin list since cookies/credentials are used.
critical_code_review_comments: finance_utils.to_base() is small, pure, and correctly reused across finance.py/dashboard.py/clients.py/portal.py - good DRY approach for the currency conversion logic.
ExpenseCreate/InvoiceCreate Pydantic models correctly validate currency against SUPPORTED_CURRENCIES and expense_type against EXPENSE_TYPES with proper 400 responses.

notes.py correctly scopes all queries by user_id (get/update/delete all filter on {'_id':..., 'user_id': user['id']}) - verified via automated test that a not-owned/nonexistent note_id returns 404, confirming per-user isolation logic is sound.
finance_summary() expense_breakdown aggregation correctly initializes all EXPENSE_TYPES keys to 0 even if no expenses exist yet, so frontend chart doesn't need to guard against missing keys.
Frontend Finance.jsx conversion rate field correctly auto-disables/resets to 1 when currency=INR and shows live '≈ ₹X in base currency' preview when USD - good UX touch, verified working in browser.
InvoiceDetail correctly displays 'Currency: USD · Rate: 1 USD = ₹83' and keeps Subtotal/Tax/Total in the invoice's own currency (not converted) - matches spec exactly.
Minor: no DELETE endpoint for portal-created client-role users (see backend_issues minor).

success_rate.backend: 97.5% (39/40 tests passing; 1 pre-existing unrelated CORS wildcard test failure carried over from prior iterations)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all Phase 3 UI flows + regression smoke test passing, no console errors)

seed_data_creation: All test data (TEST_ prefixed leads/clients/projects/invoices, TEST_ prefixed expenses and notes, test_portal_* users) created during this session and prior regression runs (test_won_automation.py) has been fully cleaned up. Verified via API: /api/invoices=0, /api/expenses=0, /api/clients=0, finance/summary all fields reset to 0.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 (multi-currency finance, expense tracker, Notes, Help page) is fully verified working - backend logic (to_base conversion, expense_breakdown aggregation, notes per-user scoping) and frontend UI (Finance/Invoices/Notes/Help/Settings/Clients/Analytics/Portal currency displays) all match spec exactly. DB is currently fully clean (0 clients/invoices/expenses/notes/leads) after this session's cleanup - any future testing agent starts from a clean slate. Use test_won_automation.py's lead->won pattern to create a test client fixture if invoice/client testing is needed again (no direct client create endpoint exists by design).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no blocking bugs found in Phase 3 scope. Only pre-existing minor CORS wildcard issue carried over from earlier iterations, and a minor missing DELETE endpoint for portal users (not a regression, just an API completeness gap).
